overview: Joins adjacent inline codes in text units and optionally moves leading and trailing codes from the text unit to the skeleton. Only the **source** codes are affected — this step should be used **before** any target is created in the text unit. Works only with `GenericSkeleton`-based filters.

removeLeadingTrailingCodes.help: Strips inline codes that appear at the very beginning or end of the source content in each text unit and relocates them into the surrounding skeleton. This simplifies the translatable text by removing markup that wraps the entire segment (e.g., outer `...` tags) so translators see cleaner content. Only works with filters that produce `GenericSkeleton` — skeleton-based relocation is not possible with other skeleton types.
notes: Only affects **source** codes. Must be applied **before** any target content is created in the text unit.

mergeCodes.help: Combines adjacent inline codes into a single code. For example, if a filter produces separate codes for `` and `` right next to each other, they are merged into one inline code ``. This reduces the number of codes translators must handle and reorder.

processingNotes: Only **source** codes are affected by this step — it should be placed in the pipeline **before** any step that creates target content in text units.
Leading/trailing code removal works only with `GenericSkeleton`-based filter events.
